---
title: "Task 2: Code"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 6
)
```

```{r libraries}
# Data manipulation and visualisation
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)
library(gridExtra)
library(glmnet)
library(caret)
library(gridExtra)
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(1)
```

# Functions for Later Usage

```{r}
# FULL METRICS FUNCTION
calculate_full_metrics <- function(obs, pred_prob, pred_class = ifelse(pred_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)) {
  obs_factor <- factor(obs, levels = c(0, 1))
  pred_factor <- factor(pred_class, levels = c(0, 1))
  TP <- sum(obs_factor == 1 & pred_factor == 1)
  TN <- sum(obs_factor == 0 & pred_factor == 0)
  FP <- sum(obs_factor == 0 & pred_factor == 1)
  FN <- sum(obs_factor == 1 & pred_factor == 0)
  n <- length(obs)
  accuracy <- (TP + TN) / n
  sensitivity <- ifelse(TP + FN > 0, TP / (TP + FN), 0)
  specificity <- ifelse(TN + FP > 0, TN / (TN + FP), 0)
  bal_acc <- (sensitivity + specificity) / 2
  precision <- ifelse(TP + FP > 0, TP / (TP + FP), 0)
  f1 <- ifelse(precision + sensitivity > 0, 2 * (precision * sensitivity) / (precision + sensitivity), 0)
  auc_val <- pROC::auc(factor(obs), pred_prob)
  return(data.frame(Accuracy = accuracy, Bal_Acc = bal_acc, AUC = as.numeric(auc_val), F1 = f1))
}
```

```{r}
# EVAL BAL ACC
eval_bal_acc <- function(model, test_df) {
  
  p <- predict(model, newdata = test_df, type = "response")
  pred_class <- ifelse(p > 0.5, 1, 0)
  cm <- confusionMatrix(
    factor(pred_class, levels = c(0, 1)),
    factor(test_df$label, levels = c(0, 1)),
    positive = "1"
  )
  
  return(cm$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"])
}
```

```{r}
calculate_balanced_accuracy <- function(y_true, y_pred) {
  # Convert to factors with same levels
  y_true <- factor(y_true, levels = c("0", "1"))
  y_pred <- factor(y_pred, levels = c("0", "1"))
  
  cm <- table(y_true, y_pred)
  
  # Calculate sensitivity and specificity
  sensitivity <- cm[2, 2] / sum(cm[2, ])
  specificity <- cm[1, 1] / sum(cm[1, ])
  
  balanced_acc <- (sensitivity + specificity) / 2
  return(balanced_acc)
}

# Function to calculate univariate importance (correlation with outcome)
calculate_univariate_importance <- function(X, y) {
  y_numeric <- as.numeric(as.character(y))
  
  # Calculate correlation for each feature
  importances <- apply(X, 2, function(feature) {
    abs(cor(feature, y_numeric, method = "spearman"))
  })
  
  return(importances)
}
```

# Data Import

```{r import-data}
# Import the compressed CSV file directly
# Note: This may take a few minutes due to large file size
cat("Loading data...\n")
data <- read.csv("data-2.csv.gz")
```

## Summary Statistics

### Label Distribution

```{r}
nrow(data); ncol(data)
```


``` {r label-dist}
ggplot(data, aes(x = factor(label))) + 
  geom_bar() +
  labs(x = "Choice", y = "Count", title = "Distribution of Choices") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_color_viridis_d()
``` 

### EDA

```{r activity-by-timebin}
# Extract time bin information from feature names
# Feature names follow pattern: unit_X_bin_Y where Y is the time bin (0-4)

cat("Analyzing neural activity by time bin...\n\n")

# Get all feature names (excluding label)
feature_names <- colnames(data)[-1]

# Extract time bin from each feature name
# Pattern: unit_X_bin_Y -> extract Y
extract_bin <- function(feature_name) {
  # Split by underscore and get the last element (bin number)
  parts <- strsplit(feature_name, "_")[[1]]
  bin_num <- as.numeric(parts[length(parts)])
  return(bin_num)
}

# Create a dataframe with activity by time bin
bin_activity <- data.frame()

for (bin in 0:4) {
  # Get features for this time bin
  bin_features <- feature_names[sapply(feature_names, extract_bin) == bin]
  
  cat("Time bin", bin, ": ", length(bin_features), "features (neurons)\n")
  
  # Calculate total activity for this bin across all trials
  bin_data <- data[, bin_features, drop = FALSE]
  
  # Summary statistics
  total_spikes <- sum(bin_data)
  mean_spikes_per_trial <- mean(rowSums(bin_data))
  mean_spikes_per_neuron <- mean(colMeans(bin_data))
  sd_spikes_per_neuron <- sd(colMeans(bin_data))
  
  bin_activity <- rbind(
    bin_activity,
    data.frame(
      time_bin = bin,
      time_window = paste0(bin*100, "-", (bin+1)*100, " ms"),
      n_neurons = length(bin_features),
      total_spikes = total_spikes,
      mean_spikes_per_trial = mean_spikes_per_trial,
      mean_spikes_per_neuron = mean_spikes_per_neuron,
      sd_spikes_per_neuron = sd_spikes_per_neuron
    )
  )
}

cat("\nSummary by Time Bin:\n")
print(bin_activity)
```


```{r plot-activity-by-timebin}
# Plot 1: Total spikes per time bin
p1 <- ggplot(bin_activity, aes(x = factor(time_bin), y = total_spikes, fill = factor(time_bin))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = format(total_spikes, big.mark = ",")), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Total Neural Activity by Time Bin",
    subtitle = "Sum of all spike counts across all trials and neurons",
    x = "Time Bin",
    y = "Total Spike Count"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("0-100ms", "100-200ms", "200-300ms", "300-400ms", "400-500ms")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.35))) +  # Add 15% space at top
  scale_fill_viridis_d(guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# Plot 2: Mean spikes per trial by time bin
p2 <- ggplot(bin_activity, aes(x = factor(time_bin), y = mean_spikes_per_trial, fill = factor(time_bin))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_spikes_per_trial, 1)), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Neural Activity Per Trial by Time Bin",
    x = "Time Bin",
    y = "Mean Spikes Per Trial"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("0-100ms", "100-200ms", "200-300ms", "300-400ms", "400-500ms")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.35))) +  # Add 15% space at top
  scale_fill_viridis_d(guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# Plot 3: Mean spikes per neuron by time bin (with error bars)
p3 <- ggplot(bin_activity, aes(x = factor(time_bin), y = mean_spikes_per_neuron, fill = factor(time_bin))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_spikes_per_neuron - sd_spikes_per_neuron,
                    ymax = mean_spikes_per_neuron + sd_spikes_per_neuron),
                width = 0.2) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_spikes_per_neuron, 2)), 
          vjust = -0.5, hjust = 1.2, size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Firing Rate Per Neuron by Time Bin",
    subtitle = "Error bars show ± 1 SD",
    x = "Time Bin",
    y = "Mean Spikes Per Neuron"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("0-100ms", "100-200ms", "200-300ms", "300-400ms", "400-500ms")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.35))) +  # Add 15% space at top
  scale_fill_viridis_d(guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 9),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# Display all plots
print(p1)
print(p2)
print(p3)

# Combined plot with better spacing
png("qm-output/eda-bins.png")
grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, ncol = 1, heights = c(1.1, 1, 1.1))
dev.off()
```

```{r activity-by-timebin-and-choice}
# Analyze activity by time bin AND choice (Left vs Right)
cat("\n\nAnalyzing neural activity by time bin and choice...\n")

bin_choice_activity <- data.frame()

for (bin in 0:4) {
  bin_features <- feature_names[sapply(feature_names, extract_bin) == bin]
  bin_data <- data[, bin_features, drop = FALSE]
  
  # For each choice
  for (choice in c(0, 1)) {
    choice_trials <- data$label == choice
    choice_bin_data <- bin_data[choice_trials, , drop = FALSE]
    
    bin_choice_activity <- rbind(
      bin_choice_activity,
      data.frame(
        time_bin = bin,
        time_window = paste0(bin*100, "-", (bin+1)*100, " ms"),
        choice = ifelse(choice == 0, "Left", "Right"),
        n_trials = sum(choice_trials),
        mean_spikes_per_trial = mean(rowSums(choice_bin_data))
      )
    )
  }
}

print(bin_choice_activity)
```


```{r plot-activity-by-timebin-and-choice}
png("qm-output/eda-LR-comp.png")
# Plot: Activity by time bin, colored by choice
ggplot(bin_choice_activity, aes(x = factor(time_bin), y = mean_spikes_per_trial, 
                                  fill = choice, group = choice)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_spikes_per_trial, 1)), 
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Neural Activity by Time Bin and Choice",
    x = "Time Bin",
    y = "Mean Spikes Per Trial",
    fill = "Choice"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("0-100ms", "100-200ms", "200-300ms", "300-400ms", "400-500ms")) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Left" = "forestgreen", "Right" = "skyblue")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
dev.off()
```

```{r trial-info}
# Trial-level summaries
data$trial_total_spikes <- rowSums(data[, -1])
ggplot(data, aes(x = factor(label), y = trial_total_spikes)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Total Spike Counts by Choice",
  x = "Choice",
  y = "Total Spikes per Trial") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r feature-summaries}
# Extract feature names excluding label
feature_names <- setdiff(names(data), "label")

features <- data.frame(
  feature = feature_names,
  mean = sapply(data[feature_names], mean),
  var = sapply(data[feature_names], var),
  zeros = sapply(data[feature_names], function(x) sum(x == 0)),
  nonzeros = sapply(data[feature_names], function(x) sum(x != 0))
)
```

```{r prop-zeros}
# 2. Calculate proportion of zeros (sparsity)
features$prop_zeros <- features$zeros / (features$zeros + features$nonzeros)

# 3. Identify constant features (zero variance)
constant_features <- features$feature[features$var == 0]
length(constant_features)
```

```{r sparsity-plot}
# 4. Plot sparsity distribution
ggplot(features, aes(x = prop_zeros)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "lightblue") + 
  labs(title = "Distribution of Sparsity (Proportion of Zero Counts) Across Features") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
data_filtered <- data[, !names(data) %in% constant_features]
feature_names_filtered <- setdiff(names(data_filtered), "label")
cat("Features after removing constants:", length(feature_names_filtered), "\n")
```

```{r data-preparation}
X <- as.matrix(data[, -1])  # Remove label column
y <- factor(data$label, levels = c("0", "1"))

# Create train/test split (80/20)
train_idx <- createDataPartition(y, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
X_train <- X[train_idx, ]
y_train <- y[train_idx]
X_test <- X[-train_idx, ]
y_test <- y[-train_idx]

cat("Training set size:", nrow(X_train), "\n")
cat("Test set size:", nrow(X_test), "\n")
cat("Number of features:", ncol(X_train), "\n")
```

# Working Code for Models

### LASSO 

```{r}
data_filtered <- data[, !names(data) %in% constant_features]
feature_names_filtered <- setdiff(names(data_filtered), "label")
```

```{r}
train_idx  <- createDataPartition(data_filtered$label, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train_data <- data[train_idx, ]
test_data  <- data[-train_idx, ]
```

```{r}
train_folds <- createFolds(train_data$label, k = 5, list = TRUE, returnTrain = TRUE)
```

```{r}
cv_lasso <- cv.glmnet(as.matrix(train_data[, feature_names_filtered]),
  train_data$label,
  family = "binomial",
  alpha = 1,
  nfolds = 5)

coef_lasso <- coef(cv_lasso, s = "lambda.min")

selected_features_lasso <- rownames(coef_lasso)[which(coef_lasso != 0)]

selected_features_lasso <- setdiff(selected_features_lasso, "(Intercept)")

cat("LASSO selected", length(selected_features_lasso), "features\n")

lasso_cv_acc <- numeric(5)

for (i in 1:5) {
  cv_train_idx <- train_folds[[i]]
  cv_train <- train_data[cv_train_idx, c("label", selected_features_lasso)]
  cv_val <- train_data[-cv_train_idx, c("label", selected_features_lasso)]
  
  cv_train$label <- as.factor(cv_train$label)
  lasso_cv_model <- glm(label ~ ., data = cv_train, family = binomial)
  lasso_cv_acc[i] <- eval_bal_acc(lasso_cv_model, cv_val)
}

lasso_cv_mean <- mean(lasso_cv_acc)
lasso_cv_sd <- sd(lasso_cv_acc)

cat("LASSO CV Balanced Accuracy:", round(lasso_cv_mean, 4), "±", round(lasso_cv_sd, 4), "\n")

lasso_train <- train_data[, c("label", selected_features_lasso)]
lasso_train$label <- as.factor(lasso_train$label)
lasso_model <- glm(label ~ ., data = lasso_train, family = binomial)
lasso_test <- test_data[, c("label", selected_features_lasso)]
lasso_test_acc <- eval_bal_acc(lasso_model, lasso_test)

cat("LASSO Test Balanced Accuracy:", round(lasso_test_acc, 4), "\n")
```

### RANDOM FOREST

```{r}
rf_train <- train_data
rf_train$label <- factor(rf_train$label)

rf_model <- randomForest(label ~ ., data = rf_train,
                         importance = TRUE,
                         ntree = 200,
                         nodesize = 10)

imp_matrix <- importance(rf_model)

imp_col <- if ("MeanDecreaseAccuracy" %in% colnames(imp_matrix)) {
  "MeanDecreaseAccuracy"
  } else {
  "MeanDecreaseGini"
  }

var_importance <- imp_matrix[, imp_col]
```

```{r}
k_values <- 1:50
rf_cv_results <- data.frame(NumFeatures = integer(), CV_Mean = numeric(), CV_SD = numeric())

for (k in k_values) {
  top_rf_features_k <- names(sort(var_importance, decreasing = TRUE))[1:k]
  cv_acc_k <- numeric(5)
  
  for (i in 1:5) {
    cv_train_idx <- train_folds[[i]]
    cv_train <- train_data[cv_train_idx, c("label", top_rf_features_k)]
    cv_val <- train_data[-cv_train_idx, c("label", top_rf_features_k)]
    
    cv_train$label <- as.factor(cv_train$label)
    rf_model_k <- glm(label ~ ., data = cv_train, family = binomial)
    cv_acc_k[i] <- eval_bal_acc(rf_model_k, cv_val)
  }
  
  rf_cv_results <- rbind(rf_cv_results, data.frame(NumFeatures = k,
                                                   CV_Mean = mean(cv_acc_k),
                                                   CV_SD = sd(cv_acc_k)))
  
}

best_k_rf <- rf_cv_results$NumFeatures[which.max(rf_cv_results$CV_Mean)]
best_acc_rf_cv <- max(rf_cv_results$CV_Mean)
cat("RF Best k (CV) =", best_k_rf, "CV accuracy =", round(best_acc_rf_cv, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Plot accuracy vs number of features
ggplot(rf_cv_results, aes(x = NumFeatures, y = CV_Mean)) +
  geom_line(color = "black") +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_point(data = rf_cv_results[which.max(rf_cv_results$CV_Mean), ],
  color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Random Forest: Balanced Accuracy vs # Features",
  x = "Number of Selected Features",
  y = "5-fold CV Balanced Accuracy") +
  theme_minimal()
```

### BORUTA


```{r}
boruta_data <- train_data
boruta_data$label <- factor(boruta_data$label)
```


```{r}
cat("Running Boruta (may take several minutes)...\n")
boruta_result <- Boruta(
  label ~ .,
  data = boruta_data,
  doTrace = 1,
  maxRuns = 50,
  pValue = 0.05
)
```


```{r}
boruta_features <- getSelectedAttributes(boruta_result, withTentative = FALSE)
cat("Boruta confirmed", length(boruta_features), "important features\n")

boruta_cv_acc <- numeric(5)

for (i in 1:5) {
  cv_train_idx <- train_folds[[i]]
  cv_train <- train_data[cv_train_idx, c("label", boruta_features)]
  cv_val <- train_data[-cv_train_idx, c("label", boruta_features)]
  
  cv_train$label <- as.factor(cv_train$label)
  boruta_cv_model <- glm(label ~ ., data = cv_train, family = binomial)
  boruta_cv_acc[i] <- eval_bal_acc(boruta_cv_model, cv_val)
}

boruta_cv_mean <- mean(boruta_cv_acc)
boruta_cv_sd <- sd(boruta_cv_acc)

cat("Boruta CV Balanced Accuracy:", round(boruta_cv_mean, 4), "±", round(boruta_cv_sd, 4), "\n")

boruta_train <- train_data[, c("label", boruta_features)]
boruta_train$label <- as.factor(boruta_train$label)
boruta_model <- glm(label ~ ., data = boruta_train, family = binomial)
boruta_test <- test_data[, c("label", boruta_features)]
boruta_test_acc <- eval_bal_acc(boruta_model, boruta_test)
cat("Boruta Test Balanced Accuracy:", round(boruta_test_acc, 4), "\n")
```

```{r}
boruta_features_tent <- getSelectedAttributes(boruta_result, withTentative = TRUE)
cat("Boruta confirmed", length(boruta_features_tent), "important features\n")

boruta_tent_cv_acc <- numeric(5)

for (i in 1:5) {
  cv_train_idx <- train_folds[[i]]
  cv_train <- train_data[cv_train_idx, c("label", boruta_features_tent)]
  cv_val <- train_data[-cv_train_idx, c("label", boruta_features_tent)]
  
  cv_train$label <- as.factor(cv_train$label)
  boruta_cv_tent_model <- glm(label ~ ., data = cv_train, family = binomial)
  boruta_tent_cv_acc[i] <- eval_bal_acc(boruta_cv_tent_model, cv_val)
}

boruta_cv_tent_mean <- mean(boruta_tent_cv_acc)
boruta_cv_tent_sd <- sd(boruta_tent_cv_acc)

cat("Boruta CV Balanced Accuracy:", round(boruta_cv_tent_mean, 4), "±", round(boruta_cv_tent_sd, 4), "\n")

boruta_tent_train <- train_data[, c("label", boruta_features_tent)]
boruta_tent_train$label <- as.factor(boruta_tent_train$label)
boruta_tent_model <- glm(label ~ ., data = boruta_tent_train, family = binomial)
boruta_tent_test <- test_data[, c("label", boruta_features_tent)]
boruta_test_tent_acc <- eval_bal_acc(boruta_tent_model, boruta_tent_test)

cat("Boruta Test Balanced Accuracy:", round(boruta_test_tent_acc, 4), "\n")
```

### FORWARD STEP

```{r data-preparation}
X <- as.matrix(data[, -1])  # Remove label column
y <- factor(data$label, levels = c("0", "1"))

# Create train/test split (80/20)
train_idx <- createDataPartition(y, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
X_train <- X[train_idx, ]
y_train <- y[train_idx]
X_test <- X[-train_idx, ]
y_test <- y[-train_idx]

cat("Training set size:", nrow(X_train), "\n")
cat("Test set size:", nrow(X_test), "\n")
cat("Number of features:", ncol(X_train), "\n")
```

```{r helper-functions}
# Function to calculate balanced accuracy
calculate_balanced_accuracy <- function(y_true, y_pred) {
  # Convert to factors with same levels
  y_true <- factor(y_true, levels = c("0", "1"))
  y_pred <- factor(y_pred, levels = c("0", "1"))
  
  cm <- table(y_true, y_pred)
  
  # Calculate sensitivity and specificity
  sensitivity <- cm[2, 2] / sum(cm[2, ])
  specificity <- cm[1, 1] / sum(cm[1, ])
  
  balanced_acc <- (sensitivity + specificity) / 2
  return(balanced_acc)
}

# Function to calculate univariate importance (correlation with outcome)
calculate_univariate_importance <- function(X, y) {
  y_numeric <- as.numeric(as.character(y))
  
  # Calculate correlation for each feature
  importances <- apply(X, 2, function(feature) {
    abs(cor(feature, y_numeric, method = "spearman"))
  })
  
  return(importances)
}
```

```{r univariate-ranking}
# Calculate importance for each feature
feature_importance <- calculate_univariate_importance(X_train, y_train)

# Create ranking dataframe
feature_ranking <- data.frame(feature = colnames(X_train), 
                              importance = feature_importance) %>% arrange(desc(importance))

# Display top 20 features
cat("\nTop 20 Features by Univariate Importance:\n")
print(head(feature_ranking, 20))

png("qm-output/fs-importancedist.png")
# Plot distribution of importance scores
ggplot(feature_ranking, aes(x = importance)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Univariate Feature Importance",
    x = "Absolute Spearman Correlation with Label",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
dev.off()
```

```{r forward-selection}
# Define feature subset sizes to evaluate
feature_counts <- c(1, 2, 5, 10, 15, 25, 50, 75, 100, 125, 150, 200, 300, 500, 750, 1000, 1500, 2000, 3000, 5000)

# Storage for results
forward_results <- data.frame()

for (n_features in feature_counts) {
  cat("\n========================================\n")
  cat("Testing with", n_features, "features...\n")
  
  # Select top n_features based on ranking
  selected_features <- feature_ranking$feature[1:n_features]
  
  # Subset the data
  X_train_subset <- X_train[, selected_features, drop = FALSE]
  X_test_subset <- X_test[, selected_features, drop = FALSE]
  
  cat("Training data dimensions:", nrow(X_train_subset), "x", ncol(X_train_subset), "\n")
  
  # === CROSS-VALIDATION ON TRAINING SET ===
  n_folds <- 5
  folds <- createFolds(y_train, k = n_folds, list = TRUE)
  
  cv_balanced_acc <- numeric(n_folds)
  cv_train_acc <- numeric(n_folds)
  
  for (fold_i in 1:n_folds) {
    fold_idx <- folds[[fold_i]]
    
    # Split into CV train and validation
    X_cv_train <- X_train_subset[-fold_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    y_cv_train <- y_train[-fold_idx]
    X_cv_val <- X_train_subset[fold_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    y_cv_val <- y_train[fold_idx]
    
    # Create training data
    cv_train_data <- as.data.frame(X_cv_train)
    cv_train_data$y <- y_cv_train
    
    # Train model on this fold
    cv_model <- glm(y ~ ., data = cv_train_data, family = "binomial", maxit = 100)
    
    # Predictions on validation fold
    cv_val_data <- data.frame(X_cv_val)
    cv_pred_prob <- predict(cv_model, newdata = cv_val_data, type = "response")
    cv_pred_class <- factor(ifelse(cv_pred_prob > 0.5, "1", "0"), levels = c("0", "1"))
      
    # Calculate balanced accuracy
    cv_balanced_acc[fold_i] <- calculate_balanced_accuracy(y_cv_val, cv_pred_class)
      
    # Calculate training accuracy on this fold
    cv_train_pred <- ifelse(cv_model$fitted.values > 0.5, "1", "0")
    cv_train_acc[fold_i] <- mean(cv_train_pred == as.character(y_cv_train))
  }
  
  # Calculate CV statistics
  mean_cv_bal_acc <- mean(cv_balanced_acc, na.rm = TRUE)
  sd_cv_bal_acc <- sd(cv_balanced_acc, na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_cv_train_acc <- mean(cv_train_acc, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  cat("CV Balanced Accuracy: ", round(mean_cv_bal_acc, 4), "\n")
  
  # === FINAL MODEL ON FULL TRAINING SET ===
  train_data <- as.data.frame(X_train_subset)
  train_data$y <- y_train
  
  final_model <- glm(y ~ ., data = train_data, family = "binomial", maxit = 100)
  
  if (!is.null(final_model)) {
    # Make predictions on held-out test set
    test_data <- data.frame(X_test_subset)
    pred_prob <- predict(final_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
    pred_class <- factor(ifelse(pred_prob > 0.5, "1", "0"), levels = c("0", "1"))
    
    # Calculate balanced accuracy on test set
    test_bal_acc <- calculate_balanced_accuracy(y_test, pred_class)
    
    # Calculate training accuracy
    train_pred_class <- ifelse(final_model$fitted.values > 0.5, "1", "0")
    train_acc <- mean(train_pred_class == as.character(y_train))
    
    # Store results with both CV and test performance
    forward_results <- rbind(
      forward_results,
      data.frame(
        n_features = n_features,
        cv_balanced_accuracy = mean_cv_bal_acc,
        cv_balanced_accuracy_sd = sd_cv_bal_acc,
        test_balanced_accuracy = test_bal_acc,
        train_accuracy = train_acc,
        train_test_diff = train_acc - mean_cv_bal_acc
      )
    )
    
    cat("Test Balanced Accuracy:", round(test_bal_acc, 4), "\n")
    cat("Training Accuracy:", round(train_acc, 4), "\n")
  } else {
    cat("Skipping this feature count due to model failure.\n")
  }}

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("FINAL RESULTS:\n")
print(head(forward_results, 20))
```

```{r overfitting-analysis}

# Find optimal number of features
best_idx <- which.max(forward_results$cv_balanced_accuracy)
best_model <- forward_results[best_idx, ]

cat("\n=== Best Performance ===\n")
cat("Number of features:", best_model$n_features, "\n")
cat("Balanced Accuracy:", round(best_model$cv_balanced_accuracy, 4), "\n")

# Check if we have training accuracy data
forward_results_long <- forward_results %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c(cv_balanced_accuracy, train_accuracy), 
               names_to = "metric", values_to = "accuracy") %>% 
  mutate(metric = recode(metric, "train_accuracy" = "Train",
                         "cv_balanced_accuracy" = "Test (Balanced)"))
  
png('qm-output/fs-performance.png')
ggplot(forward_results_long, aes(x = log(n_features), y = accuracy, color = metric)) +
    geom_line(size = 1) + geom_point(size = 2) +
    labs(title = "Forward Selection: Training vs Test Performance",
         x = "Number of Selected Features (Log Scale)", y = "Balanced Accuracy", color = "Metric") +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Train" = "coral", "Test (Balanced)" = "steelblue")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), legend.position = "bottom")
dev.off()
```


### ELASTIC NET


```{r elastic-net-cv}
# We'll test multiple alpha values to find the best mixing parameter
alpha_values <- seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.1)  # Range of alpha values to test

#alpha_values <- .3  # Range of alpha values to test

# Storage for results
elastic_net_results <- list()

# For each alpha, perform cross-validation to find optimal lambda
for (alpha_val in alpha_values) {
  cat("\nTesting alpha =", alpha_val, "\n")
  
  # Cross-validation to find optimal lambda
  cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(
    x = X_train,
    y = y_train,
    family = "binomial",
    alpha = alpha_val,
    type.measure = "class",  # Classification error
    nfolds = 5,
    standardize = TRUE
  )
  
  # Store the CV model
  elastic_net_results[[paste0("alpha_", alpha_val)]] <- list(
    cv_fit = cv_fit,
    alpha = alpha_val,
    lambda_min = cv_fit$lambda.min,
    lambda_1se = cv_fit$lambda.1se
  )
}

png("qm-output/en-alphaME-lambda.png")
# Plot CV curves for different alpha values
par(mfrow = c(3, 3))
for (result_name in names(elastic_net_results)) {
  result <- elastic_net_results[[result_name]]
  plot(result$cv_fit, main = "")
  title(main = paste("Alpha =", result$alpha), line = 2.5)
}
dev.off()
```

```{r elastic-net-evaluate}
# Function to calculate balanced accuracy
calculate_balanced_accuracy <- function(y_true, y_pred) {
  cm <- table(y_true, y_pred)
  
  # Sensitivity (True Positive Rate) for class 1
  sensitivity <- cm[2, 2] / sum(cm[2, ])
  # Specificity (True Negative Rate) for class 0
  specificity <- cm[1, 1] / sum(cm[1, ])
  
  balanced_acc <- (sensitivity + specificity) / 2
  return(balanced_acc)
}

# Evaluate each alpha on test set
elastic_net_performance <- data.frame()

for (result_name in names(elastic_net_results)) {
  result <- elastic_net_results[[result_name]]
  
  # Predictions using lambda.min
  pred_prob <- predict(
    result$cv_fit,
    newx = X_test,
    s = "lambda.min",
    type = "response"
  )
  pred_class <- ifelse(pred_prob > 0.5, 1, 0)
  
  # Get number of selected features
  coef_min <- coef(result$cv_fit, s = "lambda.min")
  n_features_min <- sum(coef_min[-1] != 0)  # Exclude intercept
  
  # Calculate balanced accuracy
  bal_acc_min <- calculate_balanced_accuracy(y_test, pred_class)
  
  # Store results for lambda.min
  elastic_net_performance <- rbind(
    elastic_net_performance,
    data.frame(
      alpha = result$alpha,
      lambda_type = "lambda.min",
      lambda = result$lambda_min,
      n_features = n_features_min,
      balanced_accuracy = bal_acc_min
    )
  )
  
  # Predictions using lambda.1se
  pred_prob_1se <- predict(
    result$cv_fit,
    newx = X_test,
    s = "lambda.1se",
    type = "response"
  )
  pred_class_1se <- ifelse(pred_prob_1se > 0.5, 1, 0)
  
  # Get number of selected features
  coef_1se <- coef(result$cv_fit, s = "lambda.1se")
  n_features_1se <- sum(coef_1se[-1] != 0)
  
  # Calculate balanced accuracy
  bal_acc_1se <- calculate_balanced_accuracy(y_test, pred_class_1se)
  
  # Store results for lambda.1se
  elastic_net_performance <- rbind(
    elastic_net_performance,
    data.frame(
      alpha = result$alpha,
      lambda_type = "lambda.1se",
      lambda = result$lambda_1se,
      n_features = n_features_1se,
      balanced_accuracy = bal_acc_1se
    )
  )
}

print(elastic_net_performance)
```

```{r elastic-net-visualization}
png("qm-output/en-ba-vs-features.png")
# Plot: Balanced Accuracy vs Number of Features for different alpha values
ggplot(elastic_net_performance, aes(x = n_features, y = balanced_accuracy, 
                                     color = factor(alpha), 
                                     shape = lambda_type)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(alpha, lambda_type))) +
  labs(
    title = "Elastic Net: Balanced Accuracy vs Number of Features",
    x = "Number of Selected Features",
    y = "Balanced Accuracy",
    color = "Alpha (L1/L2 mix)",
    shape = "Lambda Selection"
  ) + scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
dev.off()
```

```{r elastic-net-best-model}
# Find the best performing model
best_idx <- which.max(elastic_net_performance$balanced_accuracy)
best_model_info <- elastic_net_performance[best_idx, ]

cat("\nBest Elastic Net Model:\n")
cat("Alpha:", best_model_info$alpha, "\n")
cat("Lambda:", best_model_info$lambda, "\n")
cat("Number of selected features:", best_model_info$n_features, "\n")
cat("Balanced Accuracy:", round(best_model_info$balanced_accuracy, 4), "\n")

# Get the best cv.glmnet object
best_result <- elastic_net_results[[paste0("alpha_", best_model_info$alpha)]]

# Extract and display top features
best_coef <- coef(
  best_result$cv_fit,
  s = ifelse(best_model_info$lambda_type == "lambda.min", 
             "lambda.min", "lambda.1se")
)
best_coef_df <- data.frame(
  feature = rownames(best_coef),
  coefficient = as.vector(best_coef)
) %>%
  filter(coefficient != 0, feature != "(Intercept)") %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))

cat("\nTop 20 features by absolute coefficient:\n")
print(head(best_coef_df, 20))
```

```{r elastic-net-range}
# To compare with other methods: evaluate across a range of feature counts
# We'll use the best alpha and vary lambda to get different numbers of features

best_alpha <- best_model_info$alpha
best_cv_fit <- elastic_net_results[[paste0("alpha_", best_alpha)]]$cv_fit

# Get coefficients for a range of lambda values
lambda_range <- exp(seq(log(best_cv_fit$lambda.min), 
                       log(max(best_cv_fit$lambda)), 
                       length.out = 20))

elastic_net_range_results <- data.frame()

for (lambda_val in lambda_range) {
  # Get coefficients
  coef_val <- coef(best_cv_fit, s = lambda_val)
  n_features <- sum(coef_val[-1] != 0)
  
  if (n_features > 0) {  # Only evaluate if features are selected
    # Predictions
    pred_prob <- predict(best_cv_fit, newx = X_test, s = lambda_val, type = "response")
    pred_class <- factor(ifelse(pred_prob > 0.5, "1", "0"), levels = c("0", "1"))
    
    # Balanced accuracy
    bal_acc <- calculate_balanced_accuracy(y_test, pred_class)
    
    elastic_net_range_results <- rbind(
      elastic_net_range_results,
      data.frame(
        lambda = lambda_val,
        n_features = n_features,
        balanced_accuracy = bal_acc
      )
    )
  }
}

# Plot the curve
ggplot(elastic_net_range_results, aes(x = n_features, y = balanced_accuracy)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    title = paste("Elastic Net Performance Curve (α =", best_alpha, ")"),
    x = "Number of Selected Features",
    y = "Balanced Accuracy"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Summary table of results at key feature counts
feature_milestones <- c(10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000)
milestone_results <- elastic_net_range_results %>%
  mutate(closest_milestone = sapply(n_features, function(x) {
    milestone <- feature_milestones[which.min(abs(feature_milestones - x))]
    return(milestone)
  })) %>%
  group_by(closest_milestone) %>%
  filter(abs(n_features - closest_milestone) == min(abs(n_features - closest_milestone))) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(closest_milestone, n_features, balanced_accuracy) %>%
  rename(`Target Features` = closest_milestone,
         `Actual Features` = n_features,
         `Balanced Accuracy` = balanced_accuracy)

cat("\nElastic Net Performance at Key Feature Counts:\n")
print(milestone_results)
```

# Final Output (rerun models all on same data)

### Organized Code for All Models

```{r}
cat("Loading data...\n")
data <- read.csv("data-2.csv.gz")

feature_names <- setdiff(names(data), "label")

features <- data.frame(feature = feature_names, var = sapply(data[feature_names], var))
constant_features <- features$feature[features$var == 0]
data_filtered <- data[, !names(data) %in% constant_features]

train_idx <- createDataPartition(data_filtered$label, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- data_filtered[train_idx, ]
test_data <- data_filtered[-train_idx, ]

train_folds <- createFolds(train_data$label, k = 5, list = TRUE, returnTrain = TRUE)

feature_names_filtered <- setdiff(names(data_filtered), "label")
X_train <- as.matrix(train_data[, feature_names_filtered])
X_test <- as.matrix(test_data[, feature_names_filtered])
y_train <- train_data$label
y_test <- test_data$label
```


```{r}
# LASSO
cat("1. LASSO\n")
lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train, family = "binomial", alpha = 1, nfolds = 5)
lasso_pred <- predict(lasso_cv, X_test, s = "lambda.min", type = "response")
lasso_metrics <- calculate_full_metrics(y_test, lasso_pred)
lasso_nfeat <- sum(coef(lasso_cv, s = "lambda.min")[-1] != 0)
lasso_results <- data.frame(Method = "LASSO", 
                            Num_Features = lasso_nfeat,
                            Accuracy = lasso_metrics$Accuracy,
                            Bal_Acc = lasso_metrics$Bal_Acc,
                            AUC = lasso_metrics$AUC,
                            F1 = lasso_metrics$F1)

# Random Forest Top-k (CV optimized at 10,20,50 features)
cat("2. RF Top-k\n")
set.seed(1)

rf_train <- train_data
rf_train$label <- factor(rf_train$label)

rf_model <- randomForest(label ~ ., data = rf_train,
                         importance = TRUE,
                         ntree = 200,
                         nodesize = 10)

imp_matrix <- importance(rf_model)

imp_col <- if ("MeanDecreaseAccuracy" %in% colnames(imp_matrix)) {
  "MeanDecreaseAccuracy"
} else {
  "MeanDecreaseGini"
}

var_importance <- imp_matrix[, imp_col]

k_values <- 1:50
rf_cv_results <- data.frame(NumFeatures = integer(), CV_Mean = numeric(), CV_SD = numeric())

for (k in k_values) {
  top_rf_features_k <- names(sort(var_importance, decreasing = TRUE))[1:k]
  cv_acc_k <- numeric(5)
  
  for (i in 1:5) {
    cv_train_idx <- train_folds[[i]]
    cv_train <- train_data[cv_train_idx, c("label", top_rf_features_k)]
    cv_val <- train_data[-cv_train_idx, c("label", top_rf_features_k)]
    
    cv_train$label <- as.factor(cv_train$label)
    rf_model_k <- glm(label ~ ., data = cv_train, family = binomial)
    cv_acc_k[i] <- eval_bal_acc(rf_model_k, cv_val)
  }
  
  rf_cv_results <- rbind(rf_cv_results, data.frame(NumFeatures = k,
                                                   CV_Mean = mean(cv_acc_k),
                                                   CV_SD = sd(cv_acc_k)))
  
}

best_k_rf <- rf_cv_results$NumFeatures[which.max(rf_cv_results$CV_Mean)]
best_acc_rf_cv <- max(rf_cv_results$CV_Mean)
cat("RF Best k (CV) =", best_k_rf, "CV accuracy =", round(best_acc_rf_cv, 4), "\n")

ggplot(rf_cv_results, aes(x = NumFeatures, y = CV_Mean)) +
  geom_line(color = "black") +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_point(data = rf_cv_results[which.max(rf_cv_results$CV_Mean), ],
             color = "red", size = 4) +
  labs(title = "Random Forest: Balanced Accuracy vs # Features",
       x = "Number of Selected Features",
       y = "5-fold CV Balanced Accuracy") +
  theme_minimal()

best_k_rf <- rf_cv_results$NumFeatures[which.max(rf_cv_results$CV_Mean)]
best_acc_rf_cv <- max(rf_cv_results$CV_Mean)
cat("RF Best k (CV) =", best_k_rf, "CV accuracy =", round(best_acc_rf_cv, 4), "\n")

top_features <- names(sort(var_importance, decreasing = TRUE))[1:best_k_rf]
rf_train_best <- train_data[, c("label", top_features)]
rf_model_best <- glm(label ~ ., data = rf_train_best, family = binomial)
rf_pred <- predict(rf_model_best, test_data[, c("label", top_features)], type = "response")
best_metrics_rf <- calculate_full_metrics(test_data$label, rf_pred)

rf_results <- data.frame(Method = "RF Top-k", 
                         Num_Features = best_k_rf,
                         Accuracy = best_metrics_rf$Accuracy,
                         Bal_Acc = best_metrics_rf$Bal_Acc,
                         AUC = best_metrics_rf$AUC,
                         F1 = best_metrics_rf$F1)

# Forward Stepwise (Univariate ranking)
cat("3. Forward Stepwise\n")
feature_importance <- apply(X_train, 2, function(x) abs(cor(x, as.numeric(y_train), method = "spearman")))
feature_ranking <- order(feature_importance, decreasing = TRUE)
feature_counts <- c(1, 2, 5, 10, 15, 25, 50, 75, 100, 125, 150, 200, 300)
best_k_fs <- 10; best_metrics_fs <- NULL
for (k in feature_counts) {
  selected_idx <- feature_ranking[1:k]
  X_train_sub <- X_train[, selected_idx, drop = FALSE]
  X_test_sub <- X_test[, selected_idx, drop = FALSE]
  cv_bal_acc <- numeric(5)
  for (i in 1:5) {
    cv_train_idx <- train_folds[[i]]
    cv_train_X <- X_train_sub[cv_train_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    cv_train_df <- data.frame(y = y_train[cv_train_idx], as.data.frame(cv_train_X))
    cv_val_X <- X_train_sub[-cv_train_idx, , drop = FALSE]
    cv_model <- glm(y ~ ., data = cv_train_df, family = binomial, maxit = 100)
    cv_pred <- predict(cv_model, data.frame(as.data.frame(cv_val_X)), type = "response")
    cv_bal_acc[i] <- calculate_full_metrics(y_train[-cv_train_idx], cv_pred)$Bal_Acc
  }
  if (mean(cv_bal_acc) > (ifelse(is.null(best_metrics_fs), 0, best_metrics_fs$Bal_Acc))) {
    best_k_fs <- k
    train_df <- data.frame(y = y_train, as.data.frame(X_train_sub))
    fs_model <- glm(y ~ ., data = train_df, family = binomial, maxit = 100)
    fs_pred <- predict(fs_model, data.frame(as.data.frame(X_test_sub)), type = "response")
    best_metrics_fs <- calculate_full_metrics(y_test, fs_pred)
  }
}
stepwise_results <- data.frame(Method = "Forward Stepwise", 
                               Num_Features = best_k_fs,
                               Accuracy = best_metrics_fs$Accuracy,
                               Bal_Acc = best_metrics_fs$Bal_Acc,
                               AUC = best_metrics_fs$AUC,
                               F1 = best_metrics_fs$F1)


# Elastic Net (Best alpha)
cat("4. Elastic Net\n")
alpha_values <- c(0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9)
best_alpha_en <- 0.1; best_metrics_en <- NULL; best_nfeat_en <- 0
for (alpha in alpha_values) {
  en_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train, family = "binomial", alpha = alpha, nfolds = 5)
  en_pred <- predict(en_cv, X_test, s = "lambda.min", type = "response")
  en_metrics <- calculate_full_metrics(y_test, en_pred)
  if (en_metrics$Bal_Acc > (ifelse(is.null(best_metrics_en), 0, best_metrics_en$Bal_Acc))) {
    best_alpha_en <- alpha
    best_metrics_en <- en_metrics
    best_nfeat_en <- sum(coef(en_cv, s = "lambda.min")[-1] != 0)
  }
}
en_results <- data.frame(Method = "Elastic Net", 
                         Num_Features = best_nfeat_en,
                         Accuracy = best_metrics_en$Accuracy,
                         Bal_Acc = best_metrics_en$Bal_Acc,
                         AUC = best_metrics_en$AUC,
                         F1 = best_metrics_en$F1)

# Boruta
cat("5. Boruta\n")
boruta_data <- train_data[, c("label", feature_names_filtered)]
boruta_data$label <- factor(boruta_data$label)
boruta_result <- Boruta(label ~ ., data = boruta_data, doTrace = 0, maxRuns = 50, pValue = 0.05)
boruta_features <- getSelectedAttributes(boruta_result)
boruta_train <- train_data[, c("label", boruta_features)]
boruta_model <- glm(label ~ ., data = boruta_train, family = binomial)
boruta_test <- test_data[, c("label", boruta_features)]
boruta_pred <- predict(boruta_model, newdata = boruta_test, type = "response")
boruta_metrics <- calculate_full_metrics(boruta_test$label, boruta_pred)
boruta_results <- data.frame(Method = "Boruta", 
                             Num_Features = length(boruta_features),
                             Accuracy = boruta_metrics$Accuracy,
                             Bal_Acc = boruta_metrics$Bal_Acc,
                             AUC = boruta_metrics$AUC,
                             F1 = boruta_metrics$F1)

cat("6. Boruta (with tent) \n")
boruta_tent_features <- getSelectedAttributes(boruta_result, withTentative = TRUE)
boruta_tent_train <- train_data[, c("label", boruta_tent_features)]
boruta_tent_model <- glm(label ~ ., data = boruta_tent_train, family = binomial)
boruta_tent_test <- test_data[, c("label", boruta_tent_features)]
boruta_tent_pred <- predict(boruta_tent_model, newdata = boruta_tent_test, type = "response")
boruta_tent_metrics <- calculate_full_metrics(boruta_tent_test$label, boruta_tent_pred)
boruta_tent_results <- data.frame(Method = "Boruta (with Tentative)", 
                                  Num_Features = length(boruta_tent_features),
                                  Accuracy = boruta_tent_metrics$Accuracy,
                                  Bal_Acc = boruta_tent_metrics$Bal_Acc,
                                  AUC = boruta_tent_metrics$AUC,
                                  F1 = boruta_tent_metrics$F1)

# Boruta  Summary
boruta_stats <- data.frame(
  Category = c("Confirmed", "Tentative", "Rejected"),
  Count = c(sum(boruta_result$finalDecision == "Confirmed"),
            sum(boruta_result$finalDecision == "Tentative"), 
            sum(boruta_result$finalDecision == "Rejected"))
)

knitr::kable(boruta_stats)

ggplot(boruta_stats, aes(x = Category, y = Count, fill = Category)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Count), vjust = -0.3) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  labs(title = "Boruta Feature Categories", x = "Decision", y = "Number of Features") +
  theme_minimal()
```


### Final Table


```{r}


# =============================================================================
# COMBINE ALL RESULTS 
# =============================================================================

# List your results data.frames (they already have Method column):
all_model_results <- list(
  lasso_results,
  rf_results, 
  stepwise_results,
  en_results,
  boruta_results,
  boruta_tent_results  # Includes tentative
)

# Combine them all
combined_results <- bind_rows(all_model_results)

# Round numeric columns
combined_results[, c("Num_Features", "Accuracy", "Bal_Acc", "AUC", "F1")] <- 
  round(combined_results[, c("Num_Features", "Accuracy", "Bal_Acc", "AUC", "F1")], 4)
```


```{r}
# Bolding the max values
knitr::kable(combined_results, 
      digits = 4,
      caption = "Task 2: Feature Selection Results")



bold_markdown <- combined_results |>
  mutate(
    Accuracy = ifelse(Accuracy == max(Accuracy), paste0("**", Accuracy, "**"), Accuracy),
    Bal_Acc = ifelse(Bal_Acc == max(Bal_Acc), paste0("**", Bal_Acc, "**"), Bal_Acc),
    AUC = ifelse(AUC == max(AUC), paste0("**", AUC, "**"), AUC),
    F1 = ifelse(F1 == max(F1), paste0("**", F1, "**"), F1)
  )

knitr::kable(bold_markdown, 
             digits = 4,
             col.names = c("Method", "Num Features", "Accuracy", "Bal. Acc", "AUC", "F1"),
             format = "markdown")


ggplot(combined_results, aes(x = Num_Features, y = Bal_Acc, color = Method)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_label_repel(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", Bal_Acc))) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
  labs(title = "Balanced Accuracy vs Number of Selected Features",
       subtitle = "Log scale - Test set performance",
       x = "Number of Features", y = "Balanced Accuracy") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```
